title: Use race-lap ranking as a side model and stack it with binary/survival models
summary: At each race-lap, only a small subset of drivers are realistic pit candidates, so query-wise ranking is a natural adjacent formulation.
rationale: At each race-lap, only a small subset of drivers are realistic pit candidates, so query-wise ranking is a natural adjacent formulation. Ranking objectives often capture relative ordering better than plain binary loss, and a rank model is likely to diversify errors versus both binary GBDT and survival AFT. A simple OOF stacker can then exploit that diversity.
implementation_hint: Build a LightGBM `lambdarank` side model with query groups = `(Year,Race,LapNumber)` and labels = `PitNextLap`; optionally upweight positives or top positions within each query. Generate OOF predictions from three families: current best binary model, CatBoost ordered-cat model, and AFT model; then fit a fold-safe logistic or isotonic calibrator/stacker on OOF scores only.
expected_effect: Main benefit is ensemble diversification: the rank model should improve relative ordering within each lap, while the stacker converts complementary views into a modest AUC lift.
risk: Some queries have zero or many positives, so ranking setup needs care; calibration itself will not help AUC unless used as part of a genuine OOF stack.
